title: Sources, Pathways, and Impacts of Excess Fluoride in Drinking Water
description: This flowchart illustrates the natural and anthropogenic sources of fluoride, its pathway into groundwater, the resulting health and socioeconomic impacts on communities, and the various management and mitigation strategies.
relationships: Geogenic/Anthropogenic Sources release fluoride which leaches into the Groundwater Aquifer
Groundwater is extracted as Fluoride-rich Drinking Water for Human Consumption
Consumption leads to Health Impacts and subsequent Socioeconomic Impacts
Mitigation & Management Strategies are implemented to treat water and prevent exposure
